tickets.title: US001 - Persona: Student
tickets.description:
As a Student, I want to order food on my phone while walking from class, so that I can reduce the time spent waiting in the 20-minute cafeteria queue.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Verify that a student can browse the menu and place a food order via the mobile interface.
- Verify that a successfully placed order is transmitted instantly to the kitchen display screen.

tickets.title: US002 - Persona: Student
tickets.description:
As a Student with allergies, I want to filter the entire menu by checking allergen options, so that I do not see items containing ingredients that give me anxiety.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Verify that selecting an allergen filter (e.g., "No Peanuts") immediately hides all matching items from the menu.
- Verify that hidden items (like the peanut butter cookie) cannot be searched or ordered while the filter is active.

tickets.title: US003 - Persona: Student
tickets.description:
As a Student, I want to receive a push notification at 11:30 AM offering a discount on the daily special, so that I can save money on my lunch.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Verify that the system automatically triggers and delivers a push notification to students at exactly 11:30 AM.
- Verify that clicking the notification opens the app and applies the discount to the daily special item.

tickets.title: US004 - Persona: Administrator/Staff
tickets.description:
As a Chef, I want to mark any menu item as "Sold Out" instantly from the kitchen screen, so that students cannot keep ordering items we have run out of.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Verify that the Chef can select an item and toggle its status to "Sold Out" on the kitchen display.
- Verify that the status updates instantly on the student-facing app, blocking further orders for that item.

analysis.ambiguity1: "Kitchen screen showing orders": Vague because it lacks UI/functional details (e.g., chronological sorting, how to clear completed orders).
analysis.ambiguity2: "Offline sync after 10 mins": Vague because it doesn't define data conflict resolution (e.g., handling orders placed while the kitchen was offline).
analysis.conflictSolution:
Conflict: The Chef needs real-time stock control ("Sold Out"), while the Student wants a hard filter to completely hide allergen items. Completely hiding items can cause confusion if a student thinks a dish doesn't exist rather than just being unsafe or sold out.
Solution: Do not completely hide the items. Gray out and disable allergen-containing dishes, label them with a clear "Contains [Allergen]" badge, and move them to the bottom of the menu.
analysis.nfr1: Security (Data Protection & Authentication): The system must securely authenticate students (e.g., via university login) and encrypt all transaction data and personal profiles to prevent unauthorized access and protect payment information.
analysis.nfr2: Performance (Responsiveness): Must process phone orders on-the-go and reflect "instant" inventory updates.
analysis.nfr3: Usability (Accessibility): Must feature strict, anxiety-reducing menu filtering for food allergies.
